title: Snail on a pole
response:
## Working it through

The snail climbs **+4m** by day and slips **Рѕњ1m** by night, for a net of **+3m per full day-night cycle**. But the crucial detail: if it reaches the top *during* the daytime climb, it's done Рђћ it never slips that night.

Tracking its height at the start of each day (which is 3├Ќ(dayРѕњ1)):

| Day | Start | After climb (+4) | Reached 17? | After slip (Рѕњ1) |
|-----|-------|------------------|-------------|-----------------|
| 1 | 0 | 4 | no | 3 |
| 2 | 3 | 7 | no | 6 |
| 3 | 6 | 10 | no | 9 |
| 4 | 9 | 13 | no | 12 |
| 5 | 12 | 16 | no | 15 |
| 6 | 15 | **19 РЅЦ 17** | РюЁ yes | Рђћ |

On day 6 the snail starts at 15m, climbs 4m to 19m, which clears the 17m top Рђћ so it escapes before nightfall.

**Check via formula:** it needs a morning height P with P + 4 РЅЦ 17, i.e. P РЅЦ 13. Morning height is 3(nРѕњ1), so 3(nРѕњ1) РЅЦ 13 Рєњ nРѕњ1 РЅЦ 4.33 Рєњ n РЅЦ 6. РюЊ

ANSWER: 6
